steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content:
To display zeros directly, look for a factored form. Find two numbers that multiply to $168$ and add to $-46$. The pair is $-4$ and $-42$.
$$x^2-46x+168=(x-4)(x-42)$$

steps.title: Use the zero form
steps.content: A factored form displays the zeros because each factor can be set equal to $0$. $$x-4=0\quad\text{or}\quad x-42=0$$ So the zeros are visible as $4$ and $42$. Among the choices, that factored form is $(x-4)(x-42)$. The answer is B.
